objects.title: Bernoulli law \(\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\)
objects.paper_statement: We say \(A\mid X=k\sim\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\) when \(A\in\{0,1\}\) and, conditional on \(X=k\), \(P(A=1\mid X=k)=\pi_k\) and \(P(A=0\mid X=k)=1-\pi_k\).

objects.title: Finite-alphabet probability laws \(\mathsf{Prob}\)
objects.paper_statement: We say \(P\in\mathsf{Prob}(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}^2)\) when \(P\) is a probability measure on the finite observation alphabet \(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,\ldots,1\}\), equivalently a nonnegative mass assignment to each cell \((k,a,y)\) whose total mass over all \(k\in\{1,\ldots,d\}\), \(a\in\{0,1\}\), and \(y\in\{0,1\}\) is one.

objects.title: Balanced sample split $I_j$
objects.paper_statement: For $j\in\{0,1\}$, the balanced deterministic half-sample index set $I_j\subseteq\{1,\ldots,n\}$ is $I_j=\{i\in\{1,\ldots,n\}: i\le \lfloor n/2\rfloor\}$ when $j=0$, and $I_j=\{i\in\{1,\ldots,n\}: \lfloor n/2\rfloor<i\le n\}$ when $j=1$.

objects.title: Centered estimator \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\)
objects.paper_statement: For observations \(O_i=(X_i,A_i,Y_i)\), \(i=1,\ldots,n\), with binary treatment \(A_i\) and binary outcome \(Y_i\), the centered estimator is \[\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}=\frac{2}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right).\]

objects.title: Split cell counts $N^{(0)}_{aky}$ and $N^{(1)}_{aky}$
objects.paper_statement: For $j\in\{0,1\}$, $k\in\{1,\ldots,d\}$, and $a,y\in\{0,1\}$, the split cell count $N^{(j)}_{aky}$ is the number of observations in the deterministic split $I_j$ with covariate value $k$, treatment value $a$, and outcome value $y$: \[N^{(j)}_{aky}:=\sum_{i\in I_j}\mathbf 1\{X_i=k,\ A_i=a,\ Y_i=y\}.\]

objects.title: Chebyshev Polynomial $T_M$
objects.paper_statement: We write $T_M$ for the degree-$M$ Chebyshev polynomial of the first kind on $\mathbb R$, characterized by $T_0(x)=1$, $T_1(x)=x$, and $T_{M+2}(x)=2xT_{M+1}(x)-T_M(x)$ for all $M\ge0$ and $x\in\mathbb R$.

objects.title: Falling factorial \((x)_r\)
objects.paper_statement: For \(x,r\in\mathbb N_0\), we write \((x)_r\) for the falling factorial of \(x\) of order \(r\), defined by \((x)_r=\prod_{\ell=0}^{r-1}(x-\ell)\), with the empty product convention \((x)_0=1\); hence \((x)_r=0\) when \(r>x\).

objects.title: Iid sampling
objects.paper_statement: The observations \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) are independent and identically distributed with single-observation law \(P\).

objects.title: Uniform overlap
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(k\) with \(p_k>0\),
\[
\epsilon\le \pi_k\le 1-\epsilon .
\]

objects.title: Consistency
objects.paper_statement:
The observed outcome satisfies
\[
Y=Y(A)
\]
almost surely.

objects.title: Conditional exchangeability
objects.paper_statement:
The binary potential outcomes satisfy
\[
(Y(0),Y(1))\perp A\mid X .
\]

objects.title: Experiment class \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\)
objects.paper_statement:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap}, the experiment class is
\[
\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}
:=
\{P^{\otimes n}: P\in\mathsf{Prob}(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}^2),\ n,d\in\mathbb N,\ 0<\epsilon\le 1/2,\ P\text{ satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap}}\}.
\]

objects.title: Overlap cone \(\mathcal C_\epsilon\)
objects.paper_statement:
The overlap-restricted cone of nonnegative four-cell vectors is
\[
\mathcal C_\epsilon
:=
\{u=(u_{00},u_{01},u_{10},u_{11})\in\mathbb R_+^4:
\epsilon(u_{00}+u_{01}+u_{10}+u_{11})
\le u_{10}+u_{11}
\le (1-\epsilon)(u_{00}+u_{01}+u_{10}+u_{11})\}.
\]

objects.title: Cell functional \(\phi(u)\)
ATE functional \(\tau(P)\)
objects.paper_statement:
The average treatment effect functional is
\[
\tau(P)
=
\sum_{k=1}^{d}p_k(\mu_{1k}-\mu_{0k})
=
\sum_{k=1}^{d}\phi(q_k).
\]

objects.title: Hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\)
objects.paper_statement:
For a sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) with \(O_i=(X_i,A_i,Y_i)\in\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,1\}\), set
\[
I_0=\{1,\ldots,\lfloor n/2\rfloor\},\qquad
I_1=\{\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1,\ldots,n\},\qquad
m_j=|I_j|,\qquad
L=\log(en).
\]
Let \(A=6\), and set
\[
\lambda_0=256,\qquad
b_0=4096,\qquad
D_A=8\log(9A/8)=8\log(27/4),
\]
and
\[
\alpha_0
=
\min\left\{
1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}
\right\}.
\]
For \(n\ge2\), define
\[
M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\},
\qquad
B(n)=b_0L/m_1.
\]
Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\),
\[
\alpha_0L\ge2,\qquad
4M(n)^2\le m_1,\qquad
4M(n)/m_1\le 3B(n)/4.
\]
For \(n\ge N_0\), write \(M=M(n)\) and \(B=B(n)\), and define the pilot-certified heavy and light category sets by
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n
=
\left\{
k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L
\right\},
\qquad
\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
For the second split, put
\[
N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky},
\qquad
N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky},
\]
and define the heavy-category ratio contribution
\[
\widehat h_k
=
\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}
\left\{
\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}
-
\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}
\right\}.
\]
Let
\[
H_M(x)=\frac{1-T_M(1-2x)}{2M^2},
\qquad
E_M(x)=\frac{H_M(x)}{x},
\qquad
G_M(x)=\frac{1-E_M(x)}{x},
\]
where \(E_M\) and \(G_M\) are interpreted by polynomial continuation at zero. For \(u=(u_{00},u_{01},u_{10},u_{11})\), set
\[
s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1},\qquad
s(u)=s_0(u)+s_1(u),\qquad
t_1(u)=u_{11},\qquad
t_0(u)=u_{01}.
\]
Define the light-cell polynomial approximation
\[
P_{M,B}(u)
=
\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B
G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)
-
\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B
G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right).
\]
Expanding \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), set
\[
\widehat P_k
=
\sum_{|r|\le M}
c_r
\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},
\qquad
\widehat T_{\mathcal H}
=
\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,
\qquad
\widehat T_{\mathcal L}
=
\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k.
\]
The truncated balanced ratio-polynomial hybrid estimator is
\[
\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}
=
\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}.
\]
For \(n<N_0\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The definition uses no overlap parameter.

objects.title: Selected estimator \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\)
objects.paper_statement:
For fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) and fixed-interior upper-bound constant \(C_\epsilon\), define \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\{1/n+d^2/(n^2\log^2 n)\}\le 1/n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2,
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. At \(n=1\), the quotient involving \(\log n\) is interpreted as zero; the asymptotic results use this selector only above their stated large-sample cutoff.

objects.title: Minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\)
Two-category witness law
objects.paper_statement:
For \(d=2\), set
\[
p_1=p_2=\frac12,\qquad
\pi_1=\epsilon,\qquad
\pi_2=1-\epsilon,\qquad
\mu_{01}=\mu_{02}=0,\qquad
\mu_{11}=1,\qquad
\mu_{12}=0.
\]
Define a full-data extension by drawing
\[
(Y(0),Y(1))=(0,\mathbf 1\{X=1\}),
\]
and then drawing \(A\mid X=k\sim\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\).

objects.title: Two-Category Confounding Witness
objects.paper_statement:
For \(\epsilon\), suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Real parameter.)} \(\epsilon\in\mathbb R\).
\item \textbf{(Positive contrast.)} \(0<\epsilon\).
\item \textbf{(Overlap range.)} \(\epsilon\le 1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Witness law.)} \(Q\) is the full-data law constructed in Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-category-witness}, and \(P\) is its observed marginal law.
\end{itemize}
Then \(Q\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:consistency} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:conditional-exchangeability}, the observed law \(P\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap} with overlap constant \(\epsilon\), and the ATE functional of Definition~\ref{obj:def:ate-functional} obeys
\[
\tau(P)=\frac12,
\qquad
E_P[Y\mid A=1]-E_P[Y\mid A=0]=\epsilon .
\]

objects.title: Light-Cell Polynomial Rate
objects.paper_statement:
Fix an overlap constant \(\epsilon\) with \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\). The hybrid estimator is computable in the real-arithmetic model: there is a universal operation-count constant \(K>0\) such that, for every \(n\) and \(d\), a real-arithmetic program reading the hybrid count vector returns \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) exactly and uses at most \(K d M(n)^4\) arithmetic and comparison operations.

Moreover, there exist constants \(C_\epsilon,c_\epsilon>0\), depending only on \(\epsilon\), such that for every sample size \(n\), alphabet size \(d\), law \(P\) on \(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,1\}\), and sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}, if
\[
d\le c_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then the light-cell contribution satisfies
\[
E_{P^{\otimes n}}\left[\left\{\widehat T_{\mathcal L}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\phi(q_k)\right\}^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

objects.title: Pilot Sandwich Bound
Heavy Cell Rate
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon>0\) and an integer \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that the following holds.  For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), every single-observation law \(P\) on \(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,1\}\), and every sample law \(\mu_n\), assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} \(n\ge N_\epsilon\).
\item \textbf{(Dimension growth.)} \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\).
\item \textbf{(Experiment class.)} \(\mu_n=P^{\otimes n}\) belongs to \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) in the sense of Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\int \left(\widehat T_{\mathcal H}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\phi(q_k)\right)^2\,d\mu_n
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

objects.title: ATE Minimax Lower Bound
objects.paper_statement:
For any \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), let \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) denote the minimax mean-squared-error risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}. There exist constants \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and a natural-number cutoff \(N_\epsilon\) such that \(a_\epsilon>0\), \(b_\epsilon>0\), and for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le b_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
one has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\ge
a_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

external_dependencies.locator: Theorem 2; Appendix C.5; Lemma 4; Appendix D.2
external_dependencies.statement:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\). Let \(\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)\) be the fixed-sample binary-outcome subclass of \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) consisting of laws \(P^{\otimes n}\) with arbitrary masses \(p_k\ge0\), \(\sum_{k=1}^d p_k=1\), propensities satisfying \(\epsilon\le\pi_k\le1-\epsilon\) on every positive-mass category, arbitrary treated response means \(\mu_{1k}\in[0,1]\), and \(\mu_{0k}=0\) for every \(k\). Define \(\psi_1(P)=\sum_{k=1}^d p_k\mu_{1k}\). There are constants \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for all \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le b_\epsilon n\log n\),
\[
\inf_{\widehat\psi}\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)}E_P[(\widehat\psi-\psi_1(P))^2]\ge a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\},
\]
where the infimum is over measurable estimators based on the fixed sample of size \(n\).

objects.title: Centered Linear MSE Bound
objects.paper_statement:
For any \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), any \(\epsilon\in\mathbb R\), any single-observation law \(P\), and any sample law \(\mu_n\), suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} \(n>0\).
\item \textbf{(Experiment class.)} \(\mu_n\) belongs to the experiment class \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) generated by \(P\), as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}.
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}
=
\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n
2(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right).
\]
Then, with \(\tau(P)\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:ate-functional},
\[
\mathbb E_{\mu_n}\left[\left(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4\left(\frac12-\epsilon\right)^2.
\]

objects.title: Bernoulli Randomization Lower Bound
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the minimax mean-squared-error risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} satisfies
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\ge \frac{1}{100n}.
\]

objects.title: Randomized Endpoint Minimax Risk
objects.paper_statement:
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the minimax mean-squared-error risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} at exact randomization satisfies
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac{1}{n}.
\]

objects.title: Combined Upper Envelope
objects.paper_statement:
Fix constants \(\epsilon,C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Interior overlap.)} \(0<\epsilon<1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Positive constants.)} \(C_\epsilon>0\) and \(\rho_\epsilon>0\).
\item \textbf{(Hybrid bound.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), if \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), then the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\end{itemize}
Then for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), let \(K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}\). Define the \(C_\epsilon,\epsilon\)-dependent selected estimator \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. This selected estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
K_\epsilon\left[
\frac1n+
\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}
\right].
\]

objects.title: Fixed-Interior Minimax Rate
objects.paper_statement:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\). Then there exist constants \(a_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon,C_\epsilon>0\) and an integer \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that the following hold.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sharp sandwich.)} For every \(n,d\), every single-observation law \(P\), and every sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}, if \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then, with \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle},
\[
a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{Q^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_Q\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(Q)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Parametric window.)} For every \(K>0\), there exists \(C_K>0\) such that, for every \(n,d\), every single-observation law \(P\), and every sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\), if \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\),
\[
d\le K\sqrt n\,\log n
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then
\[
\frac{a_\epsilon}{n}
\le
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\frac{C_K}{n}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Consistency threshold.)} For any sequences \(n_j,d_j\) with \(n_j\to\infty\), eventually \(d_j>0\), and eventually
\[
d_j\le \rho_\epsilon n_j\log n_j,
\]
one has
\[
\mathsf R_{n_j,d_j,\epsilon}\to0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{d_j}{n_j\log n_j}\to0.
\]
\end{itemize}

external_dependencies.locator: Theorem 2; Appendix C.5; Lemma 4; Appendix D.2
external_dependencies.statement:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\). Let \(\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)\) be the fixed-sample binary-outcome subclass of \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) consisting of laws \(P^{\otimes n}\) with arbitrary masses \(p_k\ge0\), \(\sum_{k=1}^d p_k=1\), propensities satisfying \(\epsilon\le\pi_k\le1-\epsilon\) on every positive-mass category, arbitrary treated response means \(\mu_{1k}\in[0,1]\), and \(\mu_{0k}=0\) for every \(k\). Define \(\psi_1(P)=\sum_{k=1}^d p_k\mu_{1k}\). There are constants \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon>0\) and \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that, for all \(n\ge N_\epsilon\) and \(d\le b_\epsilon n\log n\),
\[
\inf_{\widehat\psi}\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal D_0^{(n,d)}(\epsilon)}E_P[(\widehat\psi-\psi_1(P))^2]\ge a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\},
\]
where the infimum is over measurable estimators based on the fixed sample of size \(n\).

objects.title: Universal Hybrid Envelope
objects.paper_statement:
The following conclusions hold for the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} and the centered estimator \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:centered-estimator}, with minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Computability.)} There is a constant \(K\in\mathbb N\), \(K>0\), such that for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) there is a real-arithmetic program which, on the hybrid count input, returns exactly \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) for every sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\), and whose arithmetic/comparison operation count is at most \(K\,d\,M(n)^4\).

\item \textbf{(Fixed-overlap hybrid calibration and envelope.)} For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon>0\), \(\rho_\epsilon>0\), and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\) such that, for all \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\), \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
the hybrid estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
For the deterministic selected estimator obtained from the rule that chooses \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and otherwise chooses \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\), one also has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\]
and
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}
\left\{
\frac1n+
\min\left[
\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\right]
\right\}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Centered estimator.)} For every \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\) and every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2 .
\]

\item \textbf{(Endpoint bracket.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac1n .
\]
\end{itemize}

c2958a692ec345ef.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.IidSampling
c2958a692ec345ef.statement:
/-- The observed sample law is the `n`-fold product of the one-unit law. -/
def IidSampling {d n : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)) : Prop :=
  mu_n = productLaw P n

d3d074b41e771047.label: DiscreteLaw
d3d074b41e771047.statement:
/-- A probability law with arbitrary masses on the finite observation alphabet. -/
structure DiscreteLaw (d : ℕ) where
  pmf : PMF (Obs d) -- @realizes P(probability law on Fin d × Bool × Bool)

a9e3bbe11e514c65.label: Obs
a9e3bbe11e514c65.statement:
/-- One observed unit `(X,A,Y)`. -/
abbrev Obs (d : ℕ) := Fin d × Bool × Bool

37b330f93b3beb8b.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap
37b330f93b3beb8b.statement:
/-- Positive-mass categories have propensity in `[epsilon,1-epsilon]`. -/
def Overlap {d : ℕ} (epsilon : ℝ) (P : DiscreteLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ k, 0 < cellMass P k →
    epsilon ≤ propensity P k ∧ propensity P k ≤ 1 - epsilon

b4ccf4977e2802d5.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Consistency

041a32266d3a55fd.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ConditionalExchangeability
041a32266d3a55fd.statement:
/-- Finite conditional-independence identity `(Y(0),Y(1)) ⟂ A | X`. -/
def ConditionalExchangeability {d : ℕ} (Q : PotentialLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ k a y0 y1,
    poAtom Q k a y0 y1 *
        (∑ a' : Bool, ∑ y0' : Bool, ∑ y1' : Bool, poAtom Q k a' y0' y1') =
      (∑ a' : Bool, poAtom Q k a' y0 y1) *
        (∑ y0' : Bool, ∑ y1' : Bool, poAtom Q k a y0' y1')

0917cb0a54c22618.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ExperimentClass
0917cb0a54c22618.statement:
/-- Membership of a sample law in the unrestricted overlap experiment class. -/
structure ExperimentClass (n : ℕ) {d : ℕ} (epsilon : ℝ)
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)) : Prop where
  epsilon_pos : 0 < epsilon
    -- @realizes epsilon(strictly positive overlap constant)
  epsilon_le_half : epsilon ≤ 1 / 2
    -- @realizes epsilon(overlap constant at most 1/2)
  product_law : IidSampling P mu_n
    -- @realizes \mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}(member law is productLaw P n)
    -- @realizes O_i(i.i.d. observations governed by the product law)
  overlap : Overlap epsilon P

9a7e43740bf476b3.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.overlapCone

40f9e647edfc2b30.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellPhiOnCone
40f9e647edfc2b30.statement:
/-- The homogeneous four-cell ATE contribution on its stated overlap-cone domain. -/
noncomputable def cellPhiOnCone (epsilon : ℝ) (u : overlapCone epsilon) : ℝ :=
  cellPhi u.1

d6224b8baca53235.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional
d6224b8baca53235.statement:
noncomputable def ateFunctional {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) : ℝ :=
  ∑ k : Fin d, cellPhi (cellVector P k)

8833e339f1187e57.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.hybridEstimator
8833e339f1187e57.statement:
noncomputable def hybridEstimator {n d : ℕ} (sample : Fin n → Obs d) : ℝ :=
  max (-1) (min 1 (heavyContribution sample + lightContribution sample))

641595e258010d12.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.minimaxRisk
641595e258010d12.statement:
noncomputable def minimaxRisk (n d : ℕ) (epsilon : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  ⨅ est : {f : (Fin n → Obs d) → ℝ // Measurable f},
    worstCaseMSE n d epsilon est.1

e0f604b5cec25a0d.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.twoCategoryWitness
e0f604b5cec25a0d.statement:
/-- Explicit two-category confounding witness with `p=(1/2,1/2)` and
propensities `(epsilon,1-epsilon)`. -/
noncomputable def twoCategoryWitness (epsilon : ℝ) (h0 : 0 ≤ epsilon)
    (h1 : epsilon ≤ 1) : PotentialLaw 2 where
  pmf := PMF.ofFintype (fun z => ENNReal.ofReal (twoCategoryMass epsilon z))
    (twoCategoryMass_sum epsilon h0 h1)

bd522e40641db5b5.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.two_category_confounding
bd522e40641db5b5.statement:
/-- The explicit full-data witness is consistent, conditionally exchangeable and
overlapping, has ATE `1/2`, and naive observed contrast `epsilon`. -/
theorem two_category_confounding (epsilon : ℝ) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon ≤ 1 / 2) :
    let Q

6f33724f340a160f.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.light_cell_polynomial
6f33724f340a160f.statement:
/-- The factorial-polynomial light branch attains the fixed-interior minimax rate.
The constants are chosen before the law and are allowed to depend only on the
fixed overlap constant. -/
lemma light_cell_polynomial (epsilon : ℝ) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon ≤ 1 / 2) :
    HybridEstimatorComputable ∧
      ∃ C_epsilon c_epsilon : ℝ,
        0 < C_epsilon ∧ 0 < c_epsilon ∧
        ∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
          (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
          ExperimentClass n epsilon P mu_n →
          (d : ℝ) ≤ c_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
          componentErrorMSE mu_n lightContribution (targetLight P) ≤
            C_epsilon * minimaxRate n d

fd2963cb1718c4fa.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.pilot_sandwich
fd2963cb1718c4fa.statement:
/-- Polynomially small failure probability for the pilot heavy/light sandwich. -/
lemma pilot_sandwich :
    (∀ K c : ℝ, 0 < K → 0 < c →
        -- @realizes c(positive constant in d ≤ c n log n)
        ∃ t₀ C N, 0 < t₀ ∧ 0 < C ∧ ∀ t : ℝ, t₀ ≤ t →
            ∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
              (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
              N ≤ n → (d : ℝ) ≤ c * n * logScale n →
              IidSampling P mu_n →
              mu_n.real (pilotBadEvent P t) ≤ C * Real.rpow n (-K)) ∧
      (∀ c : ℝ, 0 < c →
        -- @realizes c(positive constant in the K=4 specialization)
        ∃ C N, 0 < C ∧
          ∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
            (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
            N ≤ n → (d : ℝ) ≤ c * n * logScale n →
            IidSampling P mu_n →
            mu_n.real (pilotBadEvent P 256) ≤ C * Real.rpow n (-4))

b48a6bf31ded9ccf.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.universal_heavy_cell_rate
b48a6bf31ded9ccf.statement:
/-- The aggregate ratio branch has the same rate on pilot-heavy categories. -/
lemma universal_heavy_cell_rate (epsilon : ℝ) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon < 1 / 2) :
    ∃ C_epsilon rho_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
      0 < C_epsilon ∧ 0 < rho_epsilon ∧
      ∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
        (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
        N_epsilon ≤ n →
        (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
        ExperimentClass n epsilon P mu_n →
        componentErrorMSE mu_n heavyContribution (targetHeavy P) ≤
          C_epsilon * minimaxRate n d

99cd82bb21c12968.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ate_lower_bound_transfer
99cd82bb21c12968.statement:
/-- Transfers the cited control-zero one-arm converse to the unrestricted ATE
minimax problem by restriction of the law class. -/
lemma ate_lower_bound_transfer {epsilon : ℝ}
    (hZeng : ZengOneArmMinimaxLower epsilon) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon < 1 / 2) :
    ∃ a_epsilon b_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
      0 < a_epsilon ∧ 0 < b_epsilon ∧
      ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < d → N_epsilon ≤ n →
        (d : ℝ) ≤ b_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
        a_epsilon * minimaxRate n d ≤ minimaxRisk n d epsilon

926bed77c2d56654.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.near_randomization_linear_upper
926bed77c2d56654.statement:
/-- All-`n,d` MSE bound for the centered estimator. -/
lemma near_randomization_linear_upper {n d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d))
    (hClass : ExperimentClass n epsilon P mu_n) (hn : 0 < n) :
    mse mu_n centeredEstimator (ateFunctional P) ≤
      1 / (n : ℝ) + 4 * (1 / 2 - epsilon) ^ 2

748c2b3a32c77e2f.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.one_category_bernoulli_lower
748c2b3a32c77e2f.statement:
/-- Explicit one-category Bernoulli two-point lower bound at randomization. -/
lemma one_category_bernoulli_lower (n d : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (hd : 0 < d) :
    -- @realizes d(positive alphabet size for the one-category witness)
    1 / (100 * (n : ℝ)) ≤ minimaxRisk n d (1 / 2)

03b073052a90f219.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.randomized_endpoint_minimax
03b073052a90f219.statement:
/-- At exact randomization the minimax risk is parametric uniformly in `d`. -/
lemma randomized_endpoint_minimax (n d : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (hd : 0 < d) :
    -- @realizes d(positive alphabet size inherited by the endpoint bracket)
    1 / (100 * (n : ℝ)) ≤ minimaxRisk n d (1 / 2) ∧
      minimaxRisk n d (1 / 2) ≤ 1 / (n : ℝ)

731aac659bc14a2a.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.combined_upper_envelope
731aac659bc14a2a.statement:
/-- The deterministic smaller-bound selector attains the combined envelope. -/
lemma combined_upper_envelope (epsilon C_epsilon rho_epsilon : ℝ)
    (N_epsilon : ℕ) (he0 : 0 < epsilon) (he1 : epsilon < 1 / 2)
    (hC : 0 < C_epsilon) (hrho : 0 < rho_epsilon)
    (hHybrid : ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < n → N_epsilon ≤ n →
      (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
      worstCaseMSE n d epsilon hybridEstimator ≤
        C_epsilon * minimaxRate n d) :
    ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < n → N_epsilon ≤ n →
      (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
      let K_epsilon

ff2d00f670543ca6.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.sharp_minimax_fixed_interior
ff2d00f670543ca6.statement:
/-- Matched fixed-interior minimax rate.  The lower half is explicitly
conditional on the cited `ZengOneArmMinimaxLower` interface. -/
theorem sharp_minimax_fixed_interior (epsilon : ℝ)
    (hZeng : ZengOneArmMinimaxLower epsilon) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon < 1 / 2) :
    ∃ a_epsilon rho_epsilon C_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
      0 < a_epsilon ∧ 0 < rho_epsilon ∧ 0 < C_epsilon ∧
      (∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
        (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
        ExperimentClass n epsilon P mu_n →
        0 < d →
        N_epsilon ≤ n →
        (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
        a_epsilon * minimaxRate n d ≤ minimaxRisk n d epsilon ∧
        minimaxRisk n d epsilon ≤ worstCaseMSE n d epsilon hybridEstimator ∧
        worstCaseMSE n d epsilon hybridEstimator ≤
          C_epsilon * minimaxRate n d) ∧
      (∀ K : ℝ, 0 < K → ∃ C_K : ℝ, 0 < C_K ∧
        ∀ (n d : ℕ) (P : DiscreteLaw d)
          (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)),
          ExperimentClass n epsilon P mu_n →
          0 < d →
          N_epsilon ≤ n →
          (d : ℝ) ≤ K * Real.sqrt n * Real.log n →
          (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
          a_epsilon / n ≤ minimaxRisk n d epsilon ∧
          minimaxRisk n d epsilon ≤ C_K / n) ∧
      (∀ ns ds : ℕ → ℕ,
        Tendsto ns atTop atTop →
        (∀ᶠ j in atTop, 0 < ds j) →
        (∀ᶠ j in atTop,
          (ds j : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * ns j * Real.log (ns j)) →
        (Tendsto (fun j => minimaxRisk (ns j) (ds j) epsilon) atTop (nhds 0) ↔
          Tendsto (fun j => (ds j : ℝ) / (ns j * Real.log (ns j)))
            atTop (nhds 0)))

b4e14f20381c8183.label: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.overlap_adaptive_universal_hybrid
b4e14f20381c8183.statement:
/-- Universal fixed-class calibration, the all-sample centered bound, the exact
randomization bracket, and the combined upper envelope.  Clause (i) includes a
finite real-arithmetic realization with the stated `O(d M^4)` operation bound;
the hybrid's type has no `epsilon` argument, realizing its no-selector claim. -/
theorem overlap_adaptive_universal_hybrid :
    HybridEstimatorComputable ∧
    (∀ epsilon : ℝ, 0 < epsilon → epsilon < 1 / 2 →
      ∃ C_epsilon rho_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
        0 < C_epsilon ∧ 0 < rho_epsilon ∧
        ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < n → 0 < d → N_epsilon ≤ n →
          (d : ℝ) ≤ rho_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
          worstCaseMSE n d epsilon hybridEstimator ≤
              C_epsilon * minimaxRate n d ∧
          minimaxRisk n d epsilon ≤
              worstCaseMSE n d epsilon
                (selectedEstimator C_epsilon epsilon) ∧
          worstCaseMSE n d epsilon
              (selectedEstimator C_epsilon epsilon) ≤
            max C_epsilon 4 *
              (1 / (n : ℝ) +
                min (d ^ 2 / ((n : ℝ) ^ 2 * (Real.log n) ^ 2))
                  ((1 / 2 - epsilon) ^ 2))) ∧
    (∀ epsilon : ℝ, 0 < epsilon → epsilon ≤ 1 / 2 →
      ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < n → 0 < d →
        worstCaseMSE n d epsilon centeredEstimator ≤
          1 / (n : ℝ) + 4 * (1 / 2 - epsilon) ^ 2) ∧
    (∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < n → 0 < d →
      1 / (100 * (n : ℝ)) ≤ minimaxRisk n d (1 / 2) ∧
        minimaxRisk n d (1 / 2) ≤ 1 / (n : ℝ))
